geography.data_source: Howrah Municipal Corporation (HMC) ward map, note: technically separate municipality from KMC but treated as part of Kolkata metropolitan zone for simulation purposes

demographics.data_source: Census 2011 Howrah district tables, HMC population estimates, industrial belt worker demographic surveys

income_profile.data_source: NSSO 78th Round (2020-21) West Bengal urban tables, PLFS 2022-23, Howrah industrial belt employment data, jute mill closure impact estimates

commute_profile.data_source: Eastern Railway / South Eastern Railway suburban data, Howrah station — India's busiest railway station by footfall, Howrah Bridge crossing bottleneck adds 15-25 min to commute

sensitivity_parameters.data_source: Estimated from industrial zone + Bengal's highest political mobilisation culture, very low collective action threshold — Howrah has history of frequent bandhs and strikes, Lokniti-CSDS 2024

social_network_parameters.data_source: Estimated from industrial zone social patterns, very strong union networks, factory floor connections, highest tier2 density due to union + political party cadre presence

informal_economy_nodes.data_source: Howrah station daily passenger count — over 1 million daily passengers (Indian Railways), largest station vendor/porter/coolie cluster in India
Estimated from Shibpur industrial area (engineering and foundry cluster), Howrah industrial survey
Estimated from daily labour gathering points near Howrah station and industrial zone
Estimated from Howrah Flower Market (Mullik Ghat connected) wholesale activity, bridge-side market

tier1_agent_archetype_weights.data_source: Industrial Zone template from AGENT_SPEC.md, directly applicable — Howrah is a reference zone for this template

tier2_agent_types.data_source: Estimated from HMC ward councillors, extremely strong union presence (jute mills, engineering works), TMC/CPIM/SUCI political cadre, Eastern Railway institutional presence
